evidence_quotes.quote: I have noticed that nowadays video game companies and their publishers can decide to just destroy games, which is absurd.
If they claim I buy it, they shouldn't have a right to take it away, and make it unplayable.

paragraphs.text: I have noticed that nowadays video game companies and their publishers can decide to just destroy games, which is absurd. If they claim I buy it, they shouldn't have a right to take it away, and make it unplayable. If I buy a car, and when the car's manufacturer decides that it no longer want's to produce it and parts for it, my car should still work. But why does this differ to digital media? Why can they make their products unusable, because they decide it's unprofitable to upkeep? All I'm asking is that they have an end of life plan, which allows the consumer to keep using their product without them needing to do anything.
